channel.title: NPR Topics: Environment
channel.description: Breaking news on the environment, climate change, pollution, and endangered species. Also featuring Climate Connections, a special series on climate change co-produced by NPR and National Geographic.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In Albania, anger grows against the government for supporting a Kushner-linked luxury resort
item.description: Albania's government has given preliminary approval to plans for the luxury resort along a stretch of coastline, prompting daily protests and legal challenges by environmental groups.
item.content:encoded:
Albania's government has given preliminary approval to plans for the luxury resort along a stretch of coastline, prompting daily protests and legal challenges by environmental groups.

(Image credit: Vlasov Sulaj)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Defending the Everglades. Again.
item.description: Nearly 60 years ago, a fight over a massive Everglades airport helped reshape U.S. environmental law. Reporter Meghan Bowman reports on that battle in the podcast Defenders of the Everglades.
item.content:encoded: Nearly 60 years ago, a fight over a massive Everglades airport helped reshape U.S. environmental law. Reporter Meghan Bowman reports on that battle in the podcast Defenders of the Everglades.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: COMIC: How excessive heat kills and how to stay safe
item.description: Human bodies have a natural cooling system, but it can do only so much in high temperatures and humidity. Here's the science behind how heat kills. And how to protect yourself.
item.content:encoded: Human bodies have a natural cooling system, but it can do only so much in high temperatures and humidity. Here's the science behind how heat kills. And how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Can birds outsing traffic? Some are trying
item.description: New research from scientists at the Centre for Ecological Research in Hungary finds that some birds living in cities are changing their songs to compete with traffic and other urban noise.
item.content:encoded: New research from scientists at the Centre for Ecological Research in Hungary finds that some birds living in cities are changing their songs to compete with traffic and other urban noise.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Bulldozers in paradise: how a massive infrastructure project threatens an isolated island community
item.description: India plans to build a major port, airport and town on pristine, remote Great Nicobar Island. Supporters say it could boost trade and jobs, but critics worry about environmental damage.
item.content:encoded: India plans to build a major port, airport and town on pristine, remote Great Nicobar Island. Supporters say it could boost trade and jobs, but critics worry about environmental damage.
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: It's one of the world's most isolated islands. Here come the bulldozers
item.description: The Indian government is spending $9 billion to create a megaport, airport and city on this remote island. Critics fear the impact on pristine forests and the lives of indigenous inhabitants.
item.content:encoded:
The Indian government is spending $9 billion to create a megaport, airport and city on this remote island. Critics fear the impact on pristine forests and the lives of indigenous inhabitants.

(Image credit: Omkar Khandekar/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: The ancient plant of rivercane makes a comeback for flood control
item.description: To prevent flooding, people in Western North Carolina are turning to a traditional basket weaving ingredient.
item.content:encoded: To prevent flooding, people in Western North Carolina are turning to a traditional basket weaving ingredient.
item.dc:creator: Katie Myers

item.title: Inside the effort to save one of America's most imperiled salamanders
item.description: When a species is facing extinction, it takes an enormous human effort to stave it off. Case in point: the painstaking campaign to save the frosted flatwoods salamander.
item.content:encoded:
When a species is facing extinction, it takes an enormous human effort to stave it off. Case in point: the painstaking campaign to save the frosted flatwoods salamander.

(Image credit: Nathan Rott)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Disaster season is coming. Here are 3 things you can do to prepare
item.description: Insurance is supposed to soften the blow when homes burn or flood. With some preparation, you can better your odds of a smoother recovery if disaster strikes.
item.content:encoded:
Insurance is supposed to soften the blow when homes burn or flood. With some preparation, you can better your odds of a smoother recovery if disaster strikes.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: California chemical tank has cracked, causing state of emergency, thousands to evacuate
item.description: One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.
item.content:encoded:
One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.

(Image credit: Apu Gomes)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel
